images.caption:
Figure 1:Apple-π\bm{\pi}at a glance.Apple-π\pibenchmarks law-grounded physical intelligence in video models through chain-of-frames traces overPerception,Formulation, andDeduction.
Built on the 400-videoOrchardmechanics dataset, it combines a hybrid evaluation suite of MLLM-based and physics-law-grounded metrics to diagnose where reasoning fails.

Figure 2:Overview of Orchard.Left: Per-task data-source breakdown across simulated, self-recorded, and Internet-sourced videos. Right: Two-level task taxonomy organizing 400 cases into three single-law pillars and a multi-law composition branch.

Figure 3:Apple-π\bm{\pi}benchmark protocol.An infographic-annotated first frame and chain-of-frames prompt elicit five reasoning subtracks: Perception-Text for reading quantities, Perception-Graphic for segmenting objects, Formulation-Text for selecting laws, Formulation-Graphic for predicting target states, and Deduction for generating full dynamics.

Figure 4:Stage-, pillar-, and source-resolved analysis.(a,b) Stage-wise results show a decline from Perception to Formulation to Deduction.
(c) Pillar-wise results compare single- and multi-law cases.
(d) Source-wise results show the Sim-to-Real gap.
Figure 5:Qualitative failure analysis.The model preserves annotations and objects, but misbinds the initial-velocity cue, yielding a wrong target state and trajectory.
